summary: The agent performed a thorough read-only analysis of the interactive config wizard codebase, producing a comprehensive documentation report covering the entry point, menu/prompt flow, config storage schema and paths, and extension points for create/edit profile features. No files were modified — the output is purely analytical documentation. The agent traced the full data flow from TTY prompts through mutation (ConfigPatch) to YAML serialization and atomic disk writes.
decisions: Identified configWizardCommand() in src/cli/commands/config.ts as the primary wizard entry point rather than the CLI registration function registerConfigCommand()
Characterized the prompt system as custom-built on node:readline/promises with raw TTY keypress handling — explicitly noting the absence of third-party prompt libraries like Inquirer or Prompts
Mapped the 10-step sequential wizard flow with conditional logic gates (generic agent fields, reviewer maxPasses skip)
Identified the profile storage asymmetry: 'default' profile maps to root workflow.yaml while named profiles go to profiles/<name>/workflow.yaml
Noted that Zod is declared as a dependency but unused in the config layer — validation is hand-rolled via a custom ConfigDiagnostic system
Determined that a 'profile' is not a distinct object type but a full FullConfig stored at a profile-namespaced path
Identified that the wizard is a single 240-line monolithic function requiring extraction before profile flows can be added reusably
Catalogued existing profile CRUD functions and explicitly identified three missing operations: list, delete, and clone profiles
Determined the recommended insertion points for create-profile (new subcommand in registerConfigCommand) and edit-profile (modify configWizardCommand or create parallel function)

concerns.description: configWizardCommand() is a single ~240-line monolithic function. Any create/edit profile feature will require refactoring to extract the field-by-field prompt loop into a reusable function before implementation can proceed cleanly.

No profile listing capability exists. Building a profile selector UI requires scanning filesystem directories (.orchestra/profiles/ at both project and global scope) — this utility function is entirely missing from config-repository.ts.
Missing CRUD operations for profiles: no list, delete, or clone functions exist. Three new functions must be implemented before profile management flows are viable.

The /config slash command handler (command-handler.ts:83-87) punts on interactive editing by instructing users to run 'orchestrator config edit'. Any new create/edit profile flow via slash commands will need a design decision about inline prompts vs. redirect.

YAML field naming asymmetry: snake_case on disk (role_models, max_passes, error_handling, on_exhausted) but camelCase in TypeScript types. This codec layer must be handled correctly in any new profile serialization code.

Zod is listed as a dependency but unused in the config validation layer. The hand-rolled ConfigDiagnostic system is used instead. This inconsistency may confuse future contributors and represents dead dependency weight.

The DEFAULT_CONFIG_PROFILE = 'default' asymmetry (root workflow.yaml vs. profiles/<name>/workflow.yaml) must be explicitly handled when implementing profile listing or cloning to avoid treating the root config as a named profile incorrectly.
The wizard only reads activeProfile once at startup (line 451) and never prompts to change it. Profile selection must be added as an explicit pre-step before any profile editing flow can work.

humanAttentionReason: Before implementing create/edit profile flows, a human should decide: (1) whether to refactor configWizardCommand() into smaller reusable pieces first or build new profile flows alongside the monolith, and (2) how to handle the /config slash command integration — inline interactive prompts or redirect to CLI. These are architectural and UX decisions that shape the implementation approach and cannot be resolved by analysis alone.

reviewFindings.description: configWizardCommand() is a ~240-line monolithic function with no extracted helpers for the prompt loop. Adding create/edit profile flows requires this to be refactored into a reusable prompt-loop function first, otherwise logic will be duplicated.

Three profile CRUD operations are entirely missing from config-repository.ts: listProfiles() (scan profiles/ directories), deleteProfile(), and cloneProfile(). These must be implemented before any profile management UI is possible.

The wizard captures activeProfile once at line 451 and never allows the user to change it during the session. To support editing arbitrary profiles, a profile selector step must be inserted before the scope selection step.
Zod is declared as a dependency but not used in the config validation layer. Either remove it from package.json or migrate config-validation.ts to use Zod schemas for consistency.

The /config slash command handler explicitly defers interactive editing to the CLI (command-handler.ts:83-87). A design decision is needed on whether profile create/edit flows should be accessible via slash commands or remain CLI-only.

The DEFAULT_CONFIG_PROFILE = 'default' path asymmetry (root workflow.yaml vs. profiles/<name>/workflow.yaml) should be documented in config-repository.ts with an explicit comment, as it is a non-obvious invariant that must be respected in all new profile I/O code.
